tldr: Move from self-hosted BoxyHQ Jackson SSO to Clerk's hosted auth platform to get drop-in React UI and offload SSO operations.
why_switch: Clerk ships prebuilt React components (SignIn, UserButton) — much faster UI work
Hosted service eliminates the operational burden of running Jackson and its database
Generous free tier suits early-stage products before scale costs matter
First-class Next.js integration with middleware and server helpers
Built-in MFA, social login, and organization features without extra services
what_to_export: User accounts and profile metadata from your application database
Password hashes if you store them alongside Jackson-issued sessions
SAML SSO connection metadata (entityID, IdP certs, ACS URLs) per tenant
Roles or custom claims used by your app
Active session list (so you can plan a forced re-login)
export_method: Because BoxyHQ Jackson is a SAML/OIDC bridge rather than a full user store, most user data lives in your application database — export it directly. Pull SAML connection metadata from Jackson's admin API or database. Document SCIM directory tokens so you can rotate them on the new IdP. Consult Clerk's official import documentation for the exact user import format and any password hash schemes Clerk accepts.
import_method: Create a Clerk application, then bulk-import users via Clerk's user import workflow (refer to Clerk's official docs for the current format and supported password hash algorithms). Configure SAML SSO connections in the Clerk dashboard per organization. Wire your frontend to Clerk's React components and replace your existing login UI. For Next.js apps, install @clerk/nextjs middleware. Validate one tenant end-to-end before mass cutover.

concept_mapping.note: Re-upload IdP metadata in the Clerk dashboard
Map B2B tenants to Clerk Organizations
Confirm SCIM availability on your Clerk plan
Imported via Clerk's user import — keep your DB ID in publicMetadata
Clerk issues and verifies its own session tokens

gotchas: Password hash compatibility — verify Clerk supports your current hash format before exporting
Users may need to reset passwords if hashes can't be imported as-is
MFA factors typically need re-enrollment after migration
Re-issue SAML metadata to every enterprise customer's IdP — schedule windows in advance
SCIM tokens must be regenerated and shared with each IdP admin
Cost model flips from infra+ops (BoxyHQ) to per-MAU (Clerk) — model your steady-state pricing first
Frontend rewrite to Clerk components is non-trivial if you have heavy login customization

email.subject: Moving SSO from BoxyHQ Jackson to Clerk
email.body:
Hi team,

Planning to migrate auth from self-hosted BoxyHQ Jackson to Clerk. Main wins: prebuilt React components, hosted SSO/SCIM, and we drop the Jackson + DB infra we're maintaining today.

Plan:
1. Spin up Clerk app + Organizations matching our tenants.
2. Import users (verify password hash compatibility first).
3. Re-issue SAML metadata to enterprise customers per tenant.
4. Replace our login UI with Clerk components and ship behind a flag.

Effort estimate: 1-2 weeks. Open items: hash format compatibility, SCIM plan tier, and re-enrollment plan for MFA. Will follow up with a runbook.

Thanks
